purpose: Durably cluster RFC-0002 GitHub issue learnings so future implementation slices inspect related defects, preserve no-claim boundaries, and update context or skills when repeated patterns recur.

patterns.title: GitHub issue state, closure wording, docs, wiki, and agent-context truth must remain executable and mainline-backed.
patterns.futureAgentRule: Before changing RFC-0002 truth, run the execution ledger gate and live state audit, generate the issue execution summary instead of relying on chat or ad hoc assignee filters, preserve Keep-open wording for partial issues, ensure parent open_tracker issues carry status/tracker, and record exact PR/main/wiki/branch evidence on the owning issue.
nonClaimBoundaries: Issue state labels are not proof; they must point to PR, SHA, run, wiki, and branch evidence.
Slice 18 context updates do not complete RFC-0002 while implementation slices remain open.

patterns.title: Workbench and Gateway product-surface proof must be backed by route, panel, action, feedback, conversion-intent, and identity evidence.
patterns.futureAgentRule: Treat local/test caller fixtures and static Workbench or Gateway declarations as non-certifying until owner route proof, UI consumption proof, and production identity evidence are available; nested API route modules are inside the caller-context contract gate and must not bypass trusted-caller provenance or strict role-and-capability enforcement.
nonClaimBoundaries: Local/test caller authority fixtures are not production identity, authentication, or authorization proof.
Backend source contracts alone do not prove populated Workbench panel readiness.

patterns.title: Core, Advise, Manage, Report, Render, and Archive handoffs require owner runtime or materialization evidence before blockers clear.
patterns.futureAgentRule: Classify each downstream artifact by evidence class first: source-contract evidence can support readiness, but only accepted owner runtime receipts or owner materialization records can clear live execution, render, archive, or publication blockers. For Manage, bounded action-intake receipts may clear only the live-route blocker and must preserve action-register, rebalance, order, publication, production, and support blockers.
nonClaimBoundaries: Idea owns opportunity intent and evidence-pack request posture, not suitability approval, rebalance execution, report rendering, archive retention, or client publication.
Quarantined or fixture-backed outcome history is diagnostic and does not certify downstream persistence.

patterns.title: Data-product promotion and runtime trust telemetry require mesh catalog, policy, discovery, and supported-feature evidence to agree.
patterns.futureAgentRule: Use the shared supported-feature promotion evaluator and runtime trust telemetry posture instead of independent status counters or static catalog inclusion claims.
nonClaimBoundaries: Mesh policy or catalog source contracts alone do not certify data-product publication.
Runtime trust telemetry snapshots do not promote customer-facing supported features without current evidence and owner approvals.

patterns.title: Operations, security, lifecycle, migration, outbox, SLO, capacity, and disaster-recovery claims need protected or production-like attestations.
patterns.futureAgentRule: Separate contract-green local posture from protected execution evidence; do not clear capacity, lifecycle, migration, broker, DR, or vulnerability blockers with static files, local-only probes, or stale report-only evidence.
nonClaimBoundaries: Local/disposable database drills are not provider backup, PITR, failover, or production certification.
Static SLO, dashboard, lifecycle, or outbox contracts are non-certifying until runtime/protected evidence exists.

patterns.title: AI explanation output acceptance requires attested lotus-ai execution, model provenance, retention, and lineage evidence.
patterns.futureAgentRule: Keep deterministic fallback/local AI output and static workflow-pack registration separate from live provider attestation; only signed, source-safe, retention-bound runtime execution can clear #340.
nonClaimBoundaries: Idea does not implement lotus-ai provider execution, model approval, or provider retention authority.
Local fallback output is diagnostic and cannot certify workflow output acceptance.

patterns.title: Demo readiness, commercial proof, canonical archetypes, and full live journey claims must be implementation-backed end to end.
patterns.futureAgentRule: Treat commercial language as downstream of evidence: canonical opportunity archetypes, demo claim matrices, and live journeys must cite current implementation proof and preserve blockers where source-owner runtime evidence is absent.
nonClaimBoundaries: Research, blueprint language, and demo copy cannot promote unsupported features.
A single capability proof does not certify the full API, Gateway, Workbench, and downstream handoff journey.
